title: Active vs. Total Parameters
concept: An MoE model has huge total parameters (e.g. 140B) but small active parameters (e.g. 30B), achieving the quality of a 140B model at the speed of a 30B model.

exercises.prompt: Which type of model architecture uses specialized subnetworks so that only a fraction of the total parameters are active at one time?
exercises.explain: MoE stands for Mixture of Experts. It behaves like a team of specialists where only the best experts for the job are activated.

cards.title: Big Brain, Fast Speed
cards.body: Giant AI models are smart, but they are usually painfully slow and expensive to run. How do we keep the massive brainpower without the lag? We only wake up the parts we need!

cards.title: Active vs. Total
cards.body: Total parameters are all the brains/knowledge built into the model. Active parameters are only the specific parts that wake up and work on your current prompt. By keeping active parameters small, MoE models run incredibly fast.

cards.title: The Expert Library
cards.body: Imagine a massive library with 140 specialists (total parameters). When you ask a biology question, only the 30 biology experts (active parameters) step forward to answer. You get the wisdom of the whole library, but you only pay for the time of the 30 active helpers!

cards.title: Mixtral in Action
cards.body: Mixtral 8x7B is a popular MoE model. It has about 47 Billion total parameters. However, for any single word it generates, it only uses about 13 Billion active parameters. It runs super fast while thinking like a heavyweight!

cards.title: The Size Trap
cards.body: People think MoE models must run slowly because they are huge (like 140B). In reality, they run at the speed of their active parameters (like 30B), because the rest of the model is temporarily resting!

cards.title: Heavy vs. Agile
cards.body: Compare how standard dense models compare to Mixture of Experts (MoE) models in how they use their brainpower.

left.text: Uses all 140B parameters for every single word. Very slow and heavy.

right.text: Has 140B total parameters, but only activates 30B at a time. Super fast!

exercises.prompt: If an MoE model has 140 Billion total parameters but only uses 30 Billion active parameters at a time, what is the result?

options.text: It runs at the speed of 140B, with the quality of 30B.
It runs at the speed of 30B, with the quality of 140B.
It runs at the speed of 170B (30 + 140 combined).
It runs slowly because all 140B parameters must work at once.

exercises.explain: Total parameters represent the entire knowledge base (giving high quality), while active parameters are the only ones processed per token (giving fast speed).

exercises.prompt: True or False: An MoE model with 140B total parameters and 30B active parameters runs just as fast as a standard 30B model.
exercises.explain: True! Because only the active parameters do the computation, the model runs at the speed of the smaller active set, not the giant total set.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what controls the speed of a Mixture of Experts model.
exercises.sentence: An MoE model runs at the speed of its parameters.
exercises.explain: The speed of an MoE model depends on its active parameters, which are the only ones doing the actual calculations for a prompt.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant correctly explains the benefit of Active vs. Total parameters in MoE?

options.text: An MoE model runs all of its 140B parameters at the exact same time for every word, which makes it very slow.
An MoE model only activates a subset of its parameters (e.g., 30B) for each token, giving you fast speed while maintaining the quality of a 140B model.
An MoE model has 30B total parameters, but somehow uses 140B active parameters to run faster.

exercises.explain: Option B is correct because the AI agent explains that only the active parameters are loaded and computed for the prompt, keeping things fast. Option A is incorrect because MoE models do not run all parameters at once. Option C is incorrect because total parameters cannot be smaller than active parameters.

exercises.prompt: If you want a model that acts as smart as a giant 140 Billion parameter model, but runs as fast as a smaller 30 Billion parameter model, which MoE setup should you choose?

options.text: 30B active, 140B total (runs at 30B speed, behaves like 140B quality)
140B active, 30B total (runs at 140B speed, behaves like 30B quality)
0B active, 140B total (runs instantly with no quality)

exercises.explain: Active parameters determine the speed because only those parameters do math for your prompt. Total parameters determine the quality because the model has access to all specialized experts overall.
